name: western
setting_pool: A dusty frontier town at high noon
A sunbaked main street with wooden storefronts
A lonesome ranch on the high desert
A rail stop at the edge of the territory
A box canyon lit by orange dusk

background_pool.text: The wide-open spaces of {setting}, rutted wagon tracks cutting through hard-packed earth, heat shimmer distorting the horizon, wooden false-front buildings leaning against an empty sky
The sun-bleached expanse around {setting}, split-rail cedar fences enclosing a dusty corral, windmill skeleton creaking, dry grass and scrubland stretching to distant mesas
The intimate approach to {setting}, a crude platform of stacked ties and rough-sawn lumber, a weathered water tower, telegraph poles marching away into flat grassland
The dramatic geology of {setting}, sheer sandstone walls glowing burnt orange and deep red, boulders strewn across a sandy floor, deep shadows pooling in alcoves and overhangs
The rugged terrain surrounding {setting}, sun-bleached boulders and bleached skeletons of brush, a switchback trail climbing through foothill country, haze and distance blurring the peaks beyond

The close, sweltering interior of {setting}, rough-sawn planking and a single kerosene lamp casting a hard amber circle, dust motes suspended in the still air, the texture of grain and whorl visible in the wood
The general store counter within {setting}, bolts of calico and a jar of peppermint sticks, the shopkeeper's ledger and brass scale, dust motes turning in a blade of sunlight from the window
The livery stable at {setting}, stall partitions of rough-sawn pine, the smell of hay and horse, tack hung from pegs, a lantern throwing long shadows across the hard-packed dirt floor
The saloon bar of {setting}, a long counter of polished but scarred walnut, bottles ranked on shelves behind, a mirror tarnished with age, the piano in the corner with keys yellowed and chipped

subject_pool: a lawman with a tin star and stubble
a gunslinger in a long duster coat
a saloon keeper in a stained waistcoat
a cowhand in chaps and a dust-caked hat
a preacher with a weathered bible
a prospecter with a mule and a pickaxe
a cattle baron in tailored frontier finery
a deputy with a nervous hand near his holster
a riverboat gambler with a brocade vest and a derringer up his sleeve
a cavalry scout with a sun-cracked face and field glasses
a blacksmith with hammer-scale burns on his forearms
a saloon girl with a feathered hat and a knowing look
action_pool: rolling a cigarette with one hand
resting a hand on a holstered revolver
wiping sweat from a sunburned brow
leaning against a porch rail, watching
adjusting a saddle on a stamping horse
staring down an opponent from across the street
shuffling a deck of cards one-handed
studying distant dust on the horizon
pounding a horseshoe on an anvil, sparks flying
leaning in a doorway, fanning herself slowly
